title: Decodie Session
description: Schema for .decodie/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-NNN.json — a single coding session's learning entries.

timestamp_start.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session began.

timestamp_end.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session ended, or null if still in progress.

summary.description: Brief summary of what was covered in this session.

entries.description: Learning entries captured during this session.

id.description: Entry ID matching the corresponding index entry.

decision_type.description: Optional discriminator denormalized from the index entry. When present and set to 'overview', the entry uses the overview field shape (purpose/structure/...) instead of the default explanation shape (code_snippet/explanation/...).

code_snippet.description: Relevant code snippet that prompted or illustrates the learning. Required for non-overview entries.

explanation.description: Detailed explanation of the concept, decision, or pattern. Required for non-overview entries.

alternatives_considered.description: Other approaches that were considered and why this one was chosen. Required for non-overview entries.

key_concepts.description: Core concepts the reader should take away. Required for non-overview entries.

purpose.description: What the target code is for, in 2-4 sentences. Leads with intent. Required for overview entries.

structure.description: How the target code is organized — major sections/exports for a file, key modules for a directory, top-level layout for a project. Required for overview entries.

entry_points.description: Callable surfaces a developer would interact with (exported functions, CLI commands, HTTP routes, framework hooks). Optional on overview entries; omit when not meaningful.

dependencies.description: Notable internal or external dependencies and what they provide. Optional on overview entries; omit when nothing of structural significance applies.
